name: Butterscotch Pull Apart
recipeIngredient: 25 ounces Frozen Roll Dough, thawed until pliable
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare a 12-cup fluted pan with cooking spray.
2. Sprinkle half cup (1/2 cup) brown sugar on the bottom of the pan and top with walnuts; set aside.
3. In a small bowl, combine dry pudding mix, remaining brown sugar, and cinnamon.
4. Dip each roll in melted margarine, then roll in butterscotch mixture.
5. Arrange rolls on top of nut mixture in the pan. The pan should be filled at least half way with dough.
6. Let rise in a warm place until almost doubled in size, about 1 hour. The dough should rise to just under the top of the rim of the pan.
7. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. Bake for 35 minutes.
9. Turn the pan and remove from the pan onto a wire rack to cool completely.
Per serving: 306 Calories; 7g Fat (19% calories from fat); 7g Protein; 57g Carbohydrate; 0mg Cholesterol; 343mg Sodium. Notes: Instead of using Frozen Roll Dough, use 1 1/2 pounds of Frozen Bread Dough. Follow the recipe accordingly and cut thawed dough into 24 pieces.
